summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that attacker-controlled comment data can terminate the comment early and inject a real XML element into the serialized output.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should serialize attacker-controlled comment data into XML that reparses with a live injected `<z/>` element outside the intended comment boundary.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned upstream tag `0.9.9`, serialized the payload to `<r><!----><z/><!----></r>`, reparsed the result, and reported an injected `<z/>` count of `1` before printing the success signal.
verification_method: The verification script creates a comment with payload `--><z/><!--`, serializes the document, reparses the serialized XML with the same library, and asserts that exactly one `<z/>` element exists in the reparsed DOM.
evidence_produced_by_run: The reproduction printed `injected <z/> count after reparse: 1`.
The reproduction printed `EVOHUNT_SUCCESS: comment payload escaped its boundary and materialized a live <z/> element`.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out upstream tag `0.9.9`, and mounts that checkout into the container.
